summary: Muddy audience recording captures an above‑average show highlighted by a thrilling Werewolves finale, though keyboards are barely audible.
review: The performance is generally rated a notch above average with Jerry in good form and strong moments like the Werewolves finale and a lively Lazy Lightning/Eyes of the World stretch. The tape, recorded with a handheld microphone (likely a hat‑mic bootleg), suffers from muddy acoustics and very faint keyboards, especially on the first set. Overall fans enjoy the memories despite the technical shortcomings.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is praised (above‑average performance, standout songs) while recording quality is only fair due to muddy sound and missing keyboards; weighted average yields a solid 4‑star rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Hand‑held/hat microphone capture with typical 1978 mix; acoustics are muddy and Keith's keyboards are barely audible, especially in the first set.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set features standard‑year material; second set sounds better acoustically and ends with an awesome Werewolves finale.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Good form; voice and guitar sounded fine.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass was prominent in the mix, especially on 'Eyes of the World'.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keyboard (Keith) was barely audible or missing entirely in parts of the show.
